text: [SUMMARY] Engineer focused on solving engineering problems with artificial intelligence and embedded systems.
Have built AI pipelines for power systems, bioinformatics, and edge devices.
Capable of full-stack web development, backend integration, and ML engineering.
Enjoys bridging deep learning, machine learning with real-world engineering contexts.
Interested in scalable inference, lightweight ML systems, AI/ML pipelines.
Pursuing Bachelor of Engineering, Electronics and Communication Engineering at SRM Institute of Science and Technology.
Intelligence Engineer by trade, Intelligence Engineer by passion.
[PROJECTS]
Project: SleeQC — Resource-Adaptive Post-Quantum Cryptography on ESP32 Developed a continually developing system demonstrating resource-adaptive cryptography on an ESP32-S3 microcontroller.
SleeQC: Implemented a dynamic Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) engine that balances high security with real-time stability.
SleeQC: Integrated a TinyML decision module using TensorFlow Lite to monitor system load and autonomously switch cryptographic parameters.
SleeQC: Deployed ML-DSA-44 and ML-DSA-87 (Dilithium variants) for dynamic security scaling.
SleeQC: Resolved critical stack overflow issues and optimized system memory.
SleeQC: Developed firmware inculcating custom quantized ML algorithms trained on real-word resource activity data.
SleeQC: Ensured system reliability through task-level profiling and adaptive switching.
SleeQC: Handled complex C/C++ linker issues within the ESP-IDF framework and coordinated multi-language integration.  Link: https://github.com/Divyesh-Kamalanaban/sleeqc
Project: Gridifix - ML-Powered Fault Detection and Localization Engine for Smart Grids Designed a research pipeline for machine-learning-based grid fault detection and localization.
Gridifix: Affiliated with Divyesh's internship at NIT Trichy.
Gridifix: Generated over 21,000 simulated power grid scenarios including controlled faults and variable loads using pandapower.
Gridifix: Trained deep neural networks on 14,000 samples, achieving 95% accuracy in power flow estimation.
Gridifix: Implemented ensemble models reaching 92% fault detection and 100% localization accuracy.
Gridifix: Enhanced solver performance by integrating a DLPF-based optimization, reducing computation time by 70% compared to Newton-Raphson.  Link: https://github.com/Divyesh-Kamalanaban/gridifix
Project: Seaquenced - Identifying Taxonomy and Assessing Biodiversity from eDNA Datasets Contributed to Smart India Hackathon 2025 under the bioinformatics category.
Seaquenced: Built a classification pipeline to identify species from environmental DNA (eDNA) data using machine learning.
Seaquenced: Expanded raw dataset from 5 FASTQ species files by applying mutation-based augmentation, yielding 10,000+ synthetic samples.
Seaquenced: Quantized Nucleotide Transformer models from 32-bit to 4-bit precision, cutting GPU memory usage from 9.5GB to 2.5GB with minimal accuracy loss.
Seaquenced: Supported multi-stage collaboration including preprocessing, model training, and integration.  Link: https://github.com/Divyesh-Kamalanaban/seaquenced
[EXPERIENCE]
National Institute of Technology (NIT) Trichy — Research Intern Worked on grid-based fault simulation and machine learning model development for smart power systems.
National Institute of Technology (NIT) Trichy: Generated a large-scale dataset of grid conditions and explored differential power flow solvers.
National Institute of Technology (NIT) Trichy: Contributed to the early design of a data-driven fault detection engine.
Circle Communications — MERN Developer Intern Developed Tortovoice, CRM with invoice modules for a financial and inventory management platform.
Circle Communications: Worked with MongoDB, Express, React, and Node.js to create end-to-end solutions.
Circle Communications: Automated repetitive document workflows and integrated API-based file generation systems.  Link: https://github.com/Divyesh-Kamalanaban/Tortovoice
